etymology.description: semantic 歺(歺, "remains") + phonetic 朱(朱, "vermilion")
etymology.interpretation: The character 殊(shū, "special; different; extraordinary") combines semantic 歺(歺, "remains"), which can suggest change or end, with phonetic 朱(朱, "vermilion"), an intense, distinct color. This implies something distinct or extraordinary in nature.

examples.chinese: 他的想法非常特殊(他的想法非常特殊)。
这次的情况与往常不同(這次的情況與往常不同)，需要特别处理(需要特別處理)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè cì de qíngkuàng yǔ wǎngcháng bùtóng, xūyào tèbié chǔlǐ.
examples.english: This situation is different from usual and requires special handling.

examples.chinese: 我们之间的友谊与众不同(我們之間的友誼與眾不同)，极为珍贵(極為珍貴)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhījiān de yǒuyì yǔ zhòng bùtóng, jíwéi zhēnguì.
examples.english: The friendship between us is extraordinary and extremely precious.
